methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 1 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 3 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Nano Nuclear Energy is a public-stage company in the nuclear energy sector, currently valued at $808M after raising over $600M in total capital. The valuation universe comprises 3 public comparables (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 5 private peers including Oklo, X-Energy, Fuse Energy, NuScale Power, and Terrestrial Energy—a total of 8 observable data points across the sector.

The peer-median method proved inapplicable to this analysis. The nuclear energy sector contains only 3 public companies total, insufficient for reliable same-stage peer comparison. Filtering for adjacent-stage or similar-valuation-tier peers yielded fewer than the 3 datapoints required for statistical validity. A broader all-sector median approach was rejected as potentially misleading given the sector's specialized capital intensity and technology maturity profiles.

Without triangulation across sufficient comparable cohorts, this analysis cannot support a single point estimate or narrow range. The $808M current market valuation represents observable pricing; however, deriving an independent valuation conclusion requires either (1) expansion of the comp universe through transaction precedents or (2) bottom-up modeling of unit economics and addressable market, neither of which the available data permits. Key uncertainties remain the relative stage maturity across the 8-firm peer set, regulatory pathway risk differentials, and capital requirement trajectories specific to each company's technology approach. Further analysis would require granular operational and financial metrics from private peers.
